category_title: Students with initial anxiety and echolalia-focused language
minimal_context: The teacher and student are in the school dinner hall with a dog, attempting to play throw and catch.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our activity. We are going to play throw and catch with the dog.

STUDENT: (Withdraws from the dog) Bear.

TUTOR: What do you want to do with the ball?

STUDENT: (Talks about favourite cartoons)

TUTOR: Please stand up and throw the ball.

STUDENT: (Shows little attention to the teacher, engages in playing alone and sensory/ritualistic behaviour)

options.A: Make regulative practices explicit so the student knows exactly what they need to do to demonstrate competency and compliance.
options.B: Record the behavioral data in scatter plots and ABC charts when the intervention sessions in the dinner hall are finished.
options.C: Allow the student to choose how to structure and use the sessions, providing as much control over the content as possible.
options.D: Review a behavior contract with the student, outlining expectations and corresponding rewards, and require them to sign it with the tutor.
